title: API: fix libpod's container wait endpoint condition conversion

comments.body: This approach might not work due to https://github.com/gorilla/schema/issues/157, but let's see if the tests pass.
@jwhonce PTAL

Doesn't this break API? I think that podman v3 is using numeric values in RESTful API.
https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/9339
I think that change wasn't introduced https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/9048, but rather in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/9051.
@mheon @rhatdan @edigaryev ^^^

Here https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/9051/files#diff-58b335c77eb3163bb2dec31c4e20ba5a62bafa143a1fbafe1a0e02dc1372fa31R327-R330

The https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/9351 is not related to the "slice thing".

@edigaryev please try whether this PR works with `podman-remote`.
I got:
```
podman-remote wait df5c8da64b80
Error: failed to parse parameters for /v2.0.0/libpod/containers/df5c8da64b803ec8587919d37dd55d2443fd4c1a81319e04b72f3ae08863d89e/wait?condition=4&interval=250ms: schema: error converting value for index 0 of "condition"
```
because `podman-remote` do `/v2.0.0/libpod/containers/df5c8da64b803ec8587919d37dd55d2443fd4c1a81319e04b72f3ae08863d89e/wait?condition=4&interval=250ms`
If this is a bug we should fix it now.  No one is relying on this API yet, so I rather get it fixed.  We plan on releasing podman 3.0.1 very soon.

> If this is a bug we should fix it now. No one is relying on this API yet, so I rather get it fixed. We plan on releasing podman 3.0.1 very soon.

@rhatdan  I think there was some (probably unnecessary) change to API. I asked about it https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/9048#issuecomment-771299272.

It was done as part of `Switch podman stop/kill/wait handlers to use abi` PR.
https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/9051/files#diff-58b335c77eb3163bb2dec31c4e20ba5a62bafa143a1fbafe1a0e02dc1372fa31R327-R330
I am all in for using descriptive string instead of numbers, if it doesn't matter we change API between 3.0.0 and 3.0.1.
I am fine with that.

> please try whether this PR works with `podman-remote`

Thanks for spotting this! Do I understand correctly that there very little `podman-remote` tests, so this doesn't get catched automatically?
@edigaryev unfortunately those test probably didn't run at all because of its dependency on some other task that failed first.
we will see now.

Or maybe we could improve `ToParams()` function, the "enum" in question `define.ContainerStatus` implements `type Stringer interface`, we could add special case for types implementing `Stringer` into https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/pkg/bindings/util/util.go

so `String()` would be called on it instead of `strconv.FormatInt()`

`Test Bindings`

log contains:
```
[+0228s] • Failure [2.486 seconds]
[+0228s] Podman containers 
[+0228s] /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/pkg/bindings/test/containers_test.go:18
[+0228s]   podman wait to pause|unpause condition [It]
[+0228s]   /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/pkg/bindings/test/containers_test.go:273
[+0228s] 
[+0228s]   Expected
[+0228s]       <errorhandling.ErrorModel>: {
[+0228s]           Because: "schema: error converting value for index 0 of \"condition\"",
[+0228s]           Message: "failed to parse parameters for /v2.0.0/libpod/containers/140357e1bc9753fef84d650c2b4e72506e6220c93b3da20ae098ef559f9bd099/wait?condition=3: schema: error converting value for index 0 of \"condition\"",
[+0228s]           ResponseCode: 400,
[+0228s]       }
[+0228s]   to be nil
[+0228s] 
[+0228s]   /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/pkg/bindings/test/containers_test.go:282
```

> This approach might not work due to [gorilla/schema#157](https://github.com/gorilla/schema/issues/157), but let's see if the tests pass.

I check it via debugger and the slice is de-serialized form the query just all right.

@edigaryev  OK, `Test Bindings` and `APIv2 tests` are passing. The rest should pass too I believe. Beware, there are flakes. So if some kind of test passes on `fedora` and it fails on `ubuntu` don't be scared and ask maintainers for restart (on IRC).

I'm calling it a day
@mheon PTAL
LGTM
@baude we made some changes to `ToParam()` function, I think you are original author. Please check it.

/lgtm

Want this landed for 3.0.1
/approve
